title: primula poloninensis
intro: Primula poloninensis, also known as Polon's primrose, is a rare alpine plant species native to Poland and the Carpathian Mountains. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis, coughs, and colds, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Primula poloninensis include its ability to soothe sore throats and reduce fever. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and effects.
